title: NOC 52112 – Broadcast Technicians | GTR Immigration
description: Broadcast Technicians (NOC 52112) are vital to Canada’s media and communications industry, ensuring the seamless transmission of audio and video content across television, radio, and digital platforms. With the rise of streaming services and digital broadcasting, demand for skilled professionals in this field continues to grow across major Canadian cities. This occupation provides strong opportunities for individuals seeking Canada PR, especially through employer-driven programs and provincial nomination pathways.

classification.teer: ClassificationDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER Level2Major Group52 – Technical occupations in art, culture and sportSub-Major Group521 – Technical occupations in art and cultureMinor Group5211 – Technical occupations in motion pictures, broadcasting and the performing artsNOC Code52112

duties: Monitor and adjust audio and video signals
Set up live and recorded broadcasts
employment_requirements: College diploma in broadcasting, electronics, or related field
salary_prospects: The outlook remains moderately strong, supported by:
Continued growth of digital media platforms
Increasing demand for live and on-demand content
labour_market_demand: The demand for Broadcast Technicians in Canada is driven by:
Expansion of digital broadcasting and OTT platforms
Growth in live streaming and online media production
Increasing need for high-quality audio-visual transmission systems
🎥 Opportunities in television, radio, and digital media
